title: The Summit Where Paths Become Weather

features.name: the fork where choices merge
features.desc-on-touch: You stand at the precise point where the two possible paths converge into fog and become one; you read the moment before certainty.

features.name: the sky-cloud pool
features.desc-on-touch: A depression at the summit that holds water only on certain days; when full, it reflects a sky that doesn't quite match the one overhead.

features.name: the monument to both footsteps
features.desc-on-touch: A stone stack worn by countless hikers; each climber has added to it without knowing it marks the place where all choices become weather.

features.name: the mist-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: A carved marker that reads differently depending on the fog; clear days show one meaning, overcast days show another, rainy days show nothing at all.

features.name: the horizon-edge
features.desc-on-touch: Where land curves away and becomes sky; you can see both valleys below and understand why the choice mattered and why it doesn't.

inscription: where both paths rise to meet the same weather, and the choice becomes the cloud that holds them both

reverie.title: REVERIE — the summit where you become the weather
narr: You stand here with Leif at the top where the two
paths finally meet. The air is full of tiny drift,
dust or cloud or light—you can't tell anymore.
He's looking down at both valleys at once.
"This is the choice becoming real," he says.
Below, the fog rises. You've become part of the answer.

file.name: SummitLog_BothChoices_DayByDay_1998-2004.mp3
file.note: Field recordings from the ridge's summit: hikers' voices arriving, the moment they realize both paths converge here, the sound of choices becoming silence.

exits.label: back down the ridge where both footsteps still echo separately
onward into the mist where paths dissolve into weather
westward to the paddock far below where both choices still have shape
